title: Insertion into a binary search tree preserving the BST invariant

informal_statement.text:
Given a binary search tree whose in-order traversal is sorted strictly ascending and an integer key, return a new BST identical to the input on every existing key plus an additional node with the given key, unless the key is already present, in which case the tree is returned unchanged.

[Grid variant 002 — paired within-family control: distinct authoring emphasis on summary/scaffold hygiene, auxiliary lemmas, and scope discipline so obligations cannot smuggle hidden parameters. Algorithmic contract and oracle checks remain identical to variant 001.]
preconditions: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order traversal is strictly ascending)
required_properties: the output tree is a valid BST
the multiset of keys in the output equals the multiset of keys in the input union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the multiset of keys in the input
every key present in the input is also present in the output
edge_cases: single-node tree, key less than root
single-node tree, key greater than root
single-node tree, key equal to root (unchanged)
